answers: Our AU-JRC Senior High School is located at Gov. Pascual Avenue, Malabon City.
Arellano University's mascot is the Chief, named in honor of Cayetano S. Arellano, the first Chief Justice of the Philippines.
The AU-JRC was founded by the late Florentino Cayco Sr., the first Filipino Undersecretary of Public Instruction and illustrious educator

The AU-JRC was established in the year 1950. Side note, it was previously an extension of the Elisa Esguerra Campus, which was formerly known as Gregorio Sancianco - High School Campus. The GS-HSC was closed down in the 1980s and AU developed a new campus a few city blocks south in 2017, it was renamed as the Arellano University - Elisa Esguerra Campus. The old campus was renamed as the Jose Rizal Campus after the separation.
The current president of the school is Mr. Francisco Paulino V. Cayco, the CEO of Arellano University, whom oversees all campuses of AU. (As of March 2026)
You can contact our school via email at hs.joserizal@arellano.edu.ph or visit our Contact Us page [here](https://arellano.edu.ph/contact/jose-rizal-campus-au-malabon/).
You can contact our school via email at hs.joserizal@arellano.edu.ph or visit our Contact Us page [here](https://arellano.edu.ph/contact/jose-rizal-campus-au-malabon/).

To enroll, you need your previous report card (F-138), PSA birth certificate (Original Copy), 2x2 picture with white background (3 pieces), and a certificate of good moral character. You may also find more information about this [here](https://www.arellano.edu.ph/basic-education/senior-high-school/).

To enroll, you need your previous report card (F-138), PSA birth certificate (Original Copy), 2x2 picture with white background (3 pieces), and a certificate of good moral character. You may also find more information about this [here](https://www.arellano.edu.ph/basic-education/senior-high-school/).

You can easily navigate to our campus using Google Maps! [Click here for directions from your current location](https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?api=1&destination=Arellano+University+Jose+Rizal+Campus+Malabon).

You can easily navigate to our Senior High School using Google Maps! [Click here for directions from your current location](https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?api=1&destination=Arellano+University+Jose+Rizal+Campus+Malabon).

Our Senior High School offers Academic Tracks including STEM, ABM, HUMSS, GAS. We also offer Technical-Vocational-Livelihood (TVL) Strands, specifically for Information and Communication Technology (ICT), Home Economics (HE), and Industrial Arts. You may see more information about this [here](https://www.arellano.edu.ph/basic-education/senior-high-school/).
Our values spell out our mascot, the CHIEFS: Competence, Humility, Integrity, Equity, Fortitude, and Stewardship.
Our values spell out our mascot, the CHIEFS: Competence, Humility, Integrity, Equity, Fortitude, and Stewardship.

You can join the Arellano University Supreme Student Government (AUSSG), or academic clubs specific to your strand like the STEM Club, ABM Club, HUMSS Club, as well as various arts and sports organizations! You may check out our website with this information (Subject to AU's updation) [here](https://arellano.edu.ph/office-for-student-affairs/student-organizations/).
Students are strictly required to wear the prescribed school uniform with the official school patch and their ID at all times. Excessive unnatural hair color and wearing of slippers are not allowed.
The use of cellphones and other electronic gadgets is strictly prohibited while classes are ongoing to maintain focus and respect for the teachers.

Of course! AU-JRC is fully equipped with computer laboratories, a library with an Online Library system (Accessible through [here](https://arellanolibrary.com/webopac.asp)), and various facilities for student development.

Absolutely! As a DepEd-recognized institution, Arellano University accepts ESC (Educational Service Contracting) grants and Public School Vouchers to help subsidize Senior High School tuition fees. Find out more about our scholarships [here](https://arellano.edu.ph/admission/scholarship-programs/)!

Our school hymn is simply titled 'Arellano Hymn' (Arellano Pamantasan Naming Mahal), which we sing with pride during flag ceremonies and official events. Check it out [here](https://arellano.edu.ph/about/arellano-hymn/)!

Our school hymn is simply titled 'Arellano Hymn' (Arellano Pamantasan Naming Mahal), which we sing with pride during flag ceremonies and official events. Check it out [here](https://arellano.edu.ph/about/arellano-hymn/)!

Schedules vary depending on your specific strand and section, but they are typically divided into morning and afternoon shifts. Please consult your official registration form for your exact class hours. You may also reach out to us [here](https://arellano.edu.ph/contact/jose-rizal-campus-au-malabon/) for further inquiries!

You can visit the AU-JRC Guidance Office! Our guidance counselors are always available to assist students with their personal, educational, and career planning needs. Currently, they have no office phone numbers or emails so it's best to visit the office.
